input_row.title: Ensure 'Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials' are 'Disabled'
input_row.remediation: CAUTION: Applying changes may cause your App Service resource to restart. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings tab, under Platform settings, set SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials and FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials to Off. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to disable basic authentication for FTP: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name ftp -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites/<app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --set properties.allow=false For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to disable basic authentication for SCM: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name scm -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites/<app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --set properties.allow=false Default Value: Basic authentication is enabled by default. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-basic-auth-disable 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_deployment_slots_basic_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check the following:

1. **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
   - Use the Azure CLI commands provided in the remediation instructions to check the configuration for each App Service deployment slot.
   - Example command: 
     - To check SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials:
       ```
       az resource show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name scm --namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies --parent sites/<app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name>
       ```
     - To check FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials:
       ```
       az resource show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name ftp --namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies --parent sites/<app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name>
       ```

2. **Specific resources to enumerate:**
   - All App Services in the subscription, focusing on their deployment slots within each App Service.

3. **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
   - The property `properties.allow` for both FTP and SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials should be set to `false`.

4. **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
   - Pass: Both `properties.allow` for SCM and FTP basic authentication are set to `false` (indicating that Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials are disabled).
   - Fail: Any deployment slot where `properties.allow` for SCM or FTP basic authentication is set to `true`.

5. **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
   - The API endpoint for fetching resources would involve using the ARM template or the aforementioned Azure CLI commands to extract the respective settings for the deployment slots.

This approach allows for automated checking of the configuration across multiple App Services without requiring manual inspection.
